title: IntelliSense C4: editor completion source wiring
description:
Replace the static snowflakeCompletionSource in src/renderer/lib/editor/SqlEditor.svelte with a context-aware source that combines: (a) @codemirror/lang-sql's schema-aware completion fed from C1's cache, (b) the existing static keyword/builtin/type list as a secondary source. Implements the concentric-circle ranking from the grilling plan (Section 1: current schema's tables; Section 2: other schemas of current db with schema.table annotation; Section 3: other dbs with db.schema.table annotation; alphabetical within section).

UX: when cache miss triggers C2's lazy fetch, dropdown opens immediately with whatever is cached (or empty), shows a small spinner in the completion menu header, and updates in-place when the fetch resolves (no close+reopen of the menu).

Per-pane scoping: each pane derives its scope from its session's effective context (B3's tracked CURRENT_DATABASE/SCHEMA); changing the dropdowns in the worksheet header re-scopes completion immediately.

title: IntelliSense C3: session-open prefetch warmup
description:
On session activation (after sessions.svelte.ts opens a session for a profile), fire SHOW DATABASES followed by parallel SHOW SCHEMAS IN DATABASE <db> for every database the role can see. Writes results into C1's cache.

Total cost: 2 sequential round-trips (DBs first, then a Promise.all of schemas), regardless of account size. Covers FROM database., FROM database.schema. completion paths with zero perceived latency. Tables and columns stay lazy (C2 path).

Hook integrates with the existing sessions store onSessionOpen pattern; runs in background, does not block UI.
close_reason: C3 session-open prefetch landed at af942e1

title: IntelliSense C2: lazy-fetch dispatcher
description:
When C1's cache misses for a given path, fire the matching snowboy.schema.list* IPC, write the result back into the cache, and notify subscribers so the editor can refresh the open completion dropdown via CodeMirror's startCompletion API.

Maps cache paths to IPC calls:
  ['databases']                    -> snowboy.schema.listDatabases(sid)
  ['schemas', db]                  -> snowboy.schema.listSchemas(sid, db)
  ['tables', db, schema]           -> snowboy.schema.listObjects(sid, db, schema) filtered to tables
  ['columns', db, schema, table]   -> snowboy.schema.getColumns(sid, {db, schema, name: table, kind: 'table'})

Deduplicates in-flight requests so 5 fast keystrokes do not produce 5 IPC calls for the same path. Errors logged + cached as empty to avoid retry storms; cleared on cache invalidation.

title: IntelliSense C1: completion cache module
description:
Synchronous, profile-scoped renderer-side cache for completion items. Returns null on miss (consumer fires lazy fetch). API: get(profileId, path), set(profileId, path, items), invalidate(profileId, partialPath?), onChange(handler).

Path shape:
  ['databases']                          -> string[]
  ['schemas', '<db>']                    -> string[]
  ['tables', '<db>', '<schema>']         -> string[]
  ['columns', '<db>', '<schema>', '<t>'] -> string[]

This is the foundation that C2 (lazy fetcher), C4 (editor wiring), and C5 (Object Browser sync) all write to. Pure data layer, no IPC inside the module itself. SvelteMap-backed so onChange fires reactively for editor completion refresh.

title: e2e smoke cannot run from WSL against Windows-native Electron binary
description:
The Playwright Electron e2e (tests/e2e/specs/smoke.spec.ts) cannot launch from inside WSL when node_modules/electron is a Windows-native build (electron.exe).

Symptoms observed during C6 verification (snowboy-bny):
- Guard ON (cwd translated to C:\...): 'electron.launch: Failed to launch: Error: spawn .../electron.exe ENOENT' — the Linux parent process cannot chdir into a Windows-form cwd before exec.
- Guard OFF (Linux cwd /mnt/c/...): spawn succeeds but Playwright's Electron DevTools handshake fails with 'Process failed to launch!' — the stdio/devtools handshake does not survive the WSL->Windows interop boundary.

WSL interop is enabled and electron.exe runs standalone (v32.3.3), so the binary itself is fine; the issue is Playwright driving a Windows child from a Linux parent.

Impact: e2e gate is only runnable on the canonical native-Windows dev box. It is green there at HEAD (1f15134). No code regression — purely a harness/environment limitation.

Possible fixes (v0.2): (a) detect WSL+Windows-electron and skip with a clear message instead of failing; (b) document that e2e must run on native Windows; (c) install a Linux electron under WSL for a parallel headless run via xvfb.

title: IntelliSense C6: verification, e2e smoke confirmation, manual QA
description:
Final verification pass for the IntelliSense epic. Includes:
  - Full unit suite green across C1+C2+C3+C4+C5
  - e2e smoke still passes (renderer mounts, Snowboy heading visible)
  - Manual QA against a live PAT-authenticated session:
    * Type FROM, see databases ranked concentric-circles
    * Type FROM database., see schemas
    * Type FROM database.schema., see tables
    * Type table-alias., see columns from that table (lang-sql context-aware)
    * Type something not in cache: dropdown opens with spinner, fills in-place when fetch returns
    * Browse a table in Object Browser: typing its name in editor offers it immediately
  - Update handoff doc + tag a wave4-intellisense milestone if shipping standalone
close_reason: IntelliSense epic verified + tagged at aa047e9 (intellisense-v1, release live). Integration smoke (6 tests) green: 392 pass/0 fail across 30 files. typecheck/lint/test/build all green. e2e smoke un-runnable from WSL against Windows-native Electron (Playwright handshake fails across the WSL->Windows boundary); green on canonical native-Windows box; tracked as snowboy-6xk. C6 is additive (unit test + docs), no src/ touched, cannot affect e2e.

title: IntelliSense C5: Object Browser to completion cache sync
description:
When the user expands a TreeNode in the Object Browser, write the loaded children into C1's completion cache. This makes the editor immediately aware of any object the user has browsed, even without C2's lazy fetch.

Hook points:
  - Database node expand  -> writes ['schemas', db] entries
  - Schema node expand    -> writes ['tables', db, schema] entries
  - Table node expand     -> writes ['columns', db, schema, table] entries

Cache hits avoid C2's IPC entirely, so a user who has browsed gets zero-latency completion. Manual Refresh button in Object Browser (already wired by B1) clears the relevant slice; C5 re-populates on next browse.
close_reason: C5 Object Browser sync landed at db30dce
